items.description: General Viktor Afzalov, who assumed command in 2023, has been replaced by Colonel General Alexander Chaiko, according to RBC and the pro-war Telegram channel Fighterbomber.

items.description: Local governor says several injured in the attack with at least 10 houses struck by Russian missiles.

items.title: A European tax on oil superprofits is necessary
items.description: The war in the Middle East has reignited the debate on how to make multinationals that benefit from soaring oil prices contribute more. Rather than being forced to improvise under pressure from an Assemblée Nationale without a majority, it would be preferable for the government to push for the creation of a Europe-wide…

items.title: How child soldiers in Sudan become influencers on TikTok
items.description: Some Sudanese child soldiers have social media accounts are celebrated by millions of followers. Psychologists and other experts warn this is a dangerous trend.

items.title: Trump says U.S. will 'guide' stranded ships through the Strait. And, key Senate races to watch
items.description: President Trump announced that the U.S will "guide" stranded ships through the Strait of Hormuz. And, Democrats face an uphill battle to gain Senate control. Here are the key races to watch.

items.title: A diary of America's wars with photographer Peter van Agtmael
items.description: He's spent more than two decades documenting the realities of war, including years working alongside US soldiers in Iraq and Afghanistan. items.description: Media freedom is not a given and its absence frequently leaves communities and vulnerable individuals in danger. items.title: Drone Hits Moscow High-Rise Ahead Of WWII Parade, Ukraine Reports Deadly Russian Missile Attack
items.description: A drone hit a high-rise apartment building a few kilometers from the Kremlin, Moscow's mayor said, while Ukrainian authorities said a Russian missile attack killed five people and wounded several others in the eastern Kharkiv region.

items.title: The Iran war has strengthened Ukraine in surprising ways. Could a ceasefire with Russia be closer?
items.description: President Zelensky has been visiting the Gulf to demonstrate his country's military nous.

items.title: Putin’s Chornobyl: a Régime That Will Not Permit the Wound to Heal
items.description: From the 2022 Red Forest trenches to the St. Valentine’s 2025 drone strike, the Kremlin’s war is a war on the truth of 1986 – and the men who buried that truth are now being decorated for it.

items.title: A war in Europe drags on, and Africans pay the price
items.description: Citizens of Ghana, Kenya, and several other African nations are ending up on the front lines in Ukraine as Russia looks overseas to bolster its armed forces.
